headline: Featured Projects
introText: A selection of my work in e-commerce, public administration, and industrial software development.

developer-portfolio.title: Frontend Software Developer | Personal Portfolio Website
developer-portfolio.description: Conception, design, and development of a performant, multilingual portfolio website to demonstrate modern web technologies.
developer-portfolio.leadText: This portfolio was developed as an independent project to demonstrate modern web development best practices in a real-world application. The focus was on an uncompromisingly performant architecture, an accessible and highly responsive design, and seamless internationalization. By eschewing heavy CSS frameworks in favor of custom-tailored Styled Components, a lean, maintainable codebase was realized, completely driven by modular data structures.

challenges.title: Seamless Internationalization (i18n)
challenges.description: Integration of next-intl for a clean separation of application logic and textual content in German, English, and Danish, including dynamic path validation.

challenges.title: Component-Specific CSS-in-JS
challenges.description: Creation of a consistent design system with Styled Components using global themes for dark mode and pixel-perfect, responsive layouts.

challenges.title: Data-Driven UI Components
challenges.description: Architecture of completely modular UI sections (such as project heros, timelines, and skill grids) that generate fully automatically based on structured JSON objects.

architecture.title: Next.js Pages Router & SSR
architecture.description: Structured routing framework to ensure optimized loading times (Time to First Byte) and clean SEO structures through hybrid rendering.

architecture.title: Atomic / Modular Components
architecture.description: Strict separation of layout containers, reusable UI tiles (buttons, badges), and state-driven sections for maximum maintainability.

impact.title: Optimized Performance
impact.description: Achieving top scores in Google Lighthouse through semantic HTML, optimized asset loading, and avoiding overhead-intensive third-party libraries.

impact.title: Type-Safe Development
impact.description: Full type safety across all component props, theme definitions, and internationalization paths to prevent runtime errors.

deepDive.context: The code example illustrates the fault-tolerant component for link generation, which intercepts faulty or incomplete i18n translation keys and dynamically adjusts the UI.

zoo2-animalpark-manager.title: Fullstack Web Application | Zoo 2 Community Manager
zoo2-animalpark-manager.description: Development of a specialized, multilingual management platform for the 'Zoo 2: Animal Park' gaming community, designed for strategic competition planning and inventory management.
zoo2-animalpark-manager.leadText: As my first major recap project, I developed a data-driven planning tool for the 'Zoo 2: Animal Park' player community. The platform aggregates game data and helps clubs make informed decisions for in-game competitions. As part of a continuous modernization process, I fully migrated the application from its original Next.js Pages Router architecture to the modern Next.js App Router using strict TypeScript to maximize performance through Server Components and guarantee absolute type safety.

challenges.title: Migration to Next.js App Router & SWR
challenges.description: Successful refactoring of the application from Pages Router to App Router. Integrated SWR for highly efficient client-side data fetching, caching, and automatic revalidation in the frontend.

challenges.title: Cloud-Native Serverless Database
challenges.description: Migrated the relational data structures to TiDB Cloud, a highly available, distributed MySQL database architecture connected via Prisma ORM.

challenges.title: Optimized UX & Rich Notifications
challenges.description: Implemented an interactive UI using SweetAlert for visually appealing confirmation dialogs and Toastify for real-time feedback during user actions.

challenges.title: Serverless Deployment via Vercel
challenges.description: Architected a fully automated deployment pipeline on Vercel, including optimization of serverless functions and environment variables.

architecture.title: Next.js App Router & SWR Integration
architecture.description: Modern routing and rendering model with hybrid Server and Client Components, combined with SWR for reactive UI updates without manual state management.

architecture.title: Prisma ORM & TiDB Cloud Backend
architecture.description: Type-safe data access to a distributed MySQL instance in the cloud, optimized for high-performance joins and fast aggregations.

impact.title: Data-Driven Club Strategy
impact.description: Successfully delivered an MVP that gives clubs a significant, measurable competitive advantage through automated score-scenario calculators.

impact.title: Modern Cloud Architecture
impact.description: A robust, serverless tech stack on Vercel and TiDB Cloud, ensuring fault tolerance and error-free API data flows under TypeScript.

deepDive.context: This custom hook demonstrates SWR integration in the Zoo Manager frontend to reactively fetch competition scenario calculations and provide feedback using Toastify.

neuefische-fullstack-bootcamp.title: Full-Stack Software Development Training
neuefische-fullstack-bootcamp.description: 12-month intensive full-time advanced training in Full-Stack development, specializing in modern JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystems, cloud technologies, and AI-driven software engineering.
neuefische-fullstack-bootcamp.leadText: To specifically update my academic background in Business Informatics and my extensive enterprise experience with the latest cloud-native and JavaScript technologies, I completed this intensive 12-month Full-Stack bootcamp. The hands-on program focused on building modern single-page and server-side applications from scratch. A central highlight was implementing real-world practical projects using cutting-edge tools and integrating AI agents and APIs.

ba-online-antrag-alg.title: Fullstack Software Developer | Unemployment Benefits Online Application
ba-online-antrag-alg.description: Fullstack development and optimization of the nationwide online application process for unemployment benefits using Angular and Java.
ba-online-antrag-alg.leadText: Within this project for the Online Services of the Federal Employment Agency, the focus was on the further development of the digital application process for unemployment benefits. The core objective was to make the application workflow as performant, secure, and intuitive as possible for citizens. By leveraging modern web technologies in the frontend and a robust Java architecture in the backend, a highly available application was realized that meets strict legal requirements for data protection and accessibility in the public sector.

teccon-emobility-microservice.title: Software Developer (Fullstack) | E-Mobility Development
teccon-emobility-microservice.description: Conception, greenfield development, and implementation of a cloud-native microservices architecture and a single-page application for an innovative charging infrastructure management software.
teccon-emobility-microservice.leadText: In this forward-looking research project, I played a key role in designing and implementing a modern, event-driven cloud microservices architecture. The goal was to develop scalable management software for an innovative charging concept for electric vehicles. My responsibilities included requirements engineering, implementing autonomous services, integrating external infrastructure (charging stations, accounting systems), and building an automated testing architecture for both frontend and backend.

teccon-ba-infosysbub.leadText: Within the INFOSYSBUB release container, I worked in an interdisciplinary Scrum team to maintain and advance major core applications of the agency's customer portal, including ELAN (electronic notification for the heavy disability compensatory levy), the Entgeldatlas (salary atlas), and AVGS (activation and placement voucher). Beyond backend feature development in Java/Spring and frontend tasks in Angular, my main focus lay on adhering to strict digital accessibility guidelines (BITV) and automated CI/CD infrastructure.

teccon-ba-amm.title: Online Services Software Developer | AMM
teccon-ba-amm.description: Fullstack further development of the Labor Market Monitor (AMM) for interactive analysis and visualization of regional labor market and demographic structures.
teccon-ba-amm.leadText: In the Labor Market Monitor (AMM) project, the main focus was on delivering long-term data analysis regarding occupations, industries, labor markets, and demographics. I was responsible for implementing web-based interfaces using Python and Django. A substantial part of my work consisted of migrating legacy frontend components to newer Bootstrap versions and specific agency UI libraries to guarantee a standardized and fully accessible user experience.

challenges.title: Fullstack Development with Django
challenges.description: Creating and expanding web pages and interactive data tables within the Python/Django ecosystem utilizing PyCharm.

challenges.title: UI Refactoring & Bootstrap Upgrade

deepDive.context: This code sample shows a Django view in the AMM backend that filters statistical data from an Oracle database and preps it for template rendering.
deepDive.code:
# Django View for processing regional labor market statistics
from django.shortcuts import render
from django.views import View
from django.contrib.auth.mixins import LoginRequiredMixin
from ..models import RegionalStatistic

class LaborMarketAnalysisView(LoginRequiredMixin, View):
    template_name = 'amm/analysis_dashboard.html'

    def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        region_code = request.GET.get('region')
        industry_sector = request.GET.get('sector', 'ALL')

        # Query database with filters for demographic and industry analysis
        query_set = RegionalStatistic.objects.filter(region_id=region_code)
        
        if industry_sector != 'ALL':
            query_set = query_set.filter(sector_code=industry_sector)

        # Sort by long-term development (reporting year)
        statistics = query_set.order_by('-reporting_year')

        context = {
            'regional_data': statistics,
            'selected_region': region_code,
            'selected_sector': industry_sector,
        }
        
        # Render the template with the updated Bootstrap layout
        return render(request, self.template_name, context)

edict-monolith-apis.title: Software Engineer (B2B API Integration & Core Platform)
edict-monolith-apis.description: Further development of the central online casino platform alongside the conception and implementation of high-availability B2B interfaces for international third-party providers.
edict-monolith-apis.leadText: At the beginning of my nearly six-year tenure at edict eGaming GmbH, my primary focus was expanding the functionalities of the highly complex core platform. A major objective was the integration of prominent international B2B third-party providers and online gaming platforms (such as BWin, Blueprint, and the group's own Merkur Games) to guarantee seamless and secure real-time game state synchronization.

The primary goal was to deploy the entire, highly intricate online casino infrastructure reliably and error-free at the press of a button. To achieve this, I engineered declarative and scripted pipelines in Groovy under Jenkins, isolating core routines into versions-controlled shared files.

deepDive.context: The following example illustrates the syntax of a hybris ImpEx script used for structured web content migration and component-to-page mapping.
deepDive.code:
# ImpEx script for migrating CMS web content inside the hybris platform $contentCatalog=babyoneContentCatalog $contentCV=catalogVersion(CatalogVersion.catalog(Catalog.id('$contentCatalog')),CatalogVersion.version('Staged')) # Insert or update CMS components for flexible content layout INSERT_UPDATE CMSParagraphComponent;uid[unique=true];$contentCV[unique=true];name;content[lang=en];&componentRef ;welcomeBonusBanner;;"Welcome Bonus Banner";"

Sign up now and receive exclusive benefits!

";welcomeBonusBannerRef # Place the component on a predefined content page INSERT_UPDATE ContentPage;uid[unique=true];$contentCV[unique=true];title[lang=en];masterTemplate(uid,$contentCV) ;myAccountBonusPage;;"My Account - Loyalty Program";AccountPageTemplate

architecture.description: Central test framework that automatically injects the generated equipment summary as metadata into every reactor cabinet test run.

architecture.title: PyQt GUI / Report Engine
architecture.description: Creation of user interfaces or formatted logs for transparent validation of the hardware environment prior to test execution.

impact.title: Enhanced Audit Safety
impact.description: Guaranteed traceability regarding the exact IT equipment and software state used to validate the reactor cabinets.

impact.title: 100% Automated
impact.description: Complete elimination of manual documentation errors when capturing test computer specifications.

impact.title: Efficient Debugging
impact.description: Massive time savings during troubleshooting in the integration center thanks to directly provided system environment data.

deepDive.context: This Python script serves as an example of automated system analysis under Linux. It extracts kernel and hardware details and formats them to be attached to a PyTest log.
deepDive.code:
# Python script for automated hardware and software analysis under Linux
import platform
import sys

def get_system_equipment_summary():
    """Reads relevant system parameters from the test machine."""
    equipment_info = {
        "os_release": platform.release(),
        "architecture": platform.machine(),
        "python_version": sys.version.split()[0],
        "test_node_name": platform.node()
    }
    return equipment_info

if __name__ == "__main__":
    info = get_system_equipment_summary()
    print(f"--- EQUIPMENT REPORT FOR REAKTOR-SCHRANK-TESTS ---")
    for key, value in info.items():
        print(f"{key.upper()}: {value}")

isweb.title: Software Developer (Knowledge Management)
isweb.description: Development of dynamic web applications and complex intranet workflows based on the cross-platform COIS information system within the knowledge management sector.
isweb.leadText: At is@web GmbH Knowledge Management in Nuremberg, I worked on implementing the cross-platform COIS control system, designed to make corporate knowledge accessible regardless of time and location. Working closely with the group's internal advertising agency and direct corporate clients, my focus was on creating dynamic web interfaces and programming business-critical intranet workflows.

challenges.description: Conception and implementation of complex, automated business processes for the intranet using LotusScript and Lotus Notes Formula Language.

challenges.title: CMS Data Integration
challenges.description: Structured extraction and high-performance display of dynamic content and knowledge bases directly from the Lotus Notes content management system.

challenges.title: Pixel-Perfect Frontend
challenges.description: Creation of modern, dynamic web pages using HTML/CSS and JavaScript based on precise, creative specifications from the advertising agency.

architecture.title: Lotus Notes CMS
architecture.description: Central content management system for the structured storage, mapping, and provisioning of complex knowledge databases in the COIS system.

architecture.title: LotusScript & Formula
architecture.description: Utilization of LotusScript and Lotus Notes Formula Language to reliably program server-side intranet workflow logic.

architecture.title: Dynamic Web Stack
architecture.description: Utilization of native JavaScript, HTML, and CSS for the pixel-perfect transformation of agency layouts into functional web applications.

impact.title: Intranet Streamlined
impact.description: Successful digitization and acceleration of internal company processes through customized workflow modules.

impact.title: Knowledge Mobilized
impact.description: Ensured consistent, multilingual data availability for management, independent of time and location.

impact.title: Agency Alignment
impact.description: Seamless and smooth technical implementation of demanding design and layout specifications directly for end clients.

deepDive.context: To control knowledge release within the COIS system, server-side workflows were implemented. The following code example demonstrates the logical structure of an automated approval process in LotusScript, validating document states and triggering notifications within the intranet.
deepDive.code:
' LotusScript for automated workflow control in the intranet
Sub Initialize
    Dim session New NotesSession
    Dim db As NotesDatabase
    Dim dc As NotesDocumentCollection
    Dim doc As NotesDocument
    
    Set db = session.CurrentDatabase
    Set dc = db.UnprocessedDocuments
    Set doc = dc.GetFirstDocument
    
    While Not(doc Is Nothing)
        ' Check the current workflow state
        If doc.GetItemValue("Status")(0) = "Pending_Approval" Then
            ' Usability adjustment: Set automatic timestamp
            Call doc.ReplaceItemValue("ApprovedDate", Now)
            Call doc.ReplaceItemValue("Status", "Published")
            
            ' Hand over workflow to the COIS knowledge management system
            Call doc.Save(True, False)
        End If
        Set doc = dc.GetNextDocument(doc)
    Wend
End Sub

memphis.title: Student Assistant (Project MEMPHIS)
memphis.description: Development of a personalized, graphical user interface for PDAs. Focused on the integration and processing of XML documents.
memphis.leadText: The EU research project MEMPHIS (Multilingual Content for Flexible Format Internet Premium Services) at C-LAB – a cooperation between the University of Paderborn and Siemens AG – developed sophisticated, platform-independent online services for the media and financial sectors in collaboration with partners such as Bertelsmann. As a student assistant, I contributed to the conception of flexible user interfaces for mobile devices. Within my diploma thesis, my focus was on the methodical preparation and validation of the MEMPHIS demonstrators and prototypes according to usability criteria – laying the foundation for my current understanding of intuitive user experience.

challenges.title: Usability & Prototyping
challenges.description: Methodical preparation and validation of the mobile MEMPHIS demonstrators according to strict usability criteria as part of the diploma thesis.

challenges.title: XML Data Processing
challenges.description: Structured integration, validation, and dynamic manipulation of XML documents for flexible content delivery.

challenges.title: System Integration
challenges.description: Seamless integration of the newly developed mobile interface into the research project's pre-existing overall system.

deepDive.context: As part of prototype development, server-side transformation of XML structures via XSLT was used. The Java Servlet dynamically transformed multilingual raw data into a slim output format optimized for PDAs to ensure accessibility and usability on mobile devices.
